en.extract:
The white-bellied green pigeon (Treron sieboldii), also known as Siebold's green pigeon, is a species of bird in the family Columbidae. It is found in East, and Southeast Asia. Its natural habitat is temperate forests and woodlands. Its population has increased significantly since 2000. It is one of the few green pigeons that migrates.


Etymology
The species epithet is named after Phillip Franz von Siebold, a German naturalist and traveler.


Distribution and habitat
White-bellied green pigeons are found in Japan, eastern China and northern Southeast Asia].  In Japan, their population is found mainly in Hokkaido and the Ryukyu Islands. They also occur in Taiwan and eastern and southern China, near the coast, and in northern Thailand, Laos and Vietnam.
The species is generally sedentary, but birds in Hokkaido are wholly migratory and only present there from May to September, and the populations in the rest of Japan also undertake some poorly understood migrations. 
This pigeon is a forest dweller. It settles deciduous and mixed forests and prefers primary forests. In Japan, it exists in the forests of lowlands and in mountain forests, up to 1,400 m (4,600 ft). 


Description

The white-bellied green pigeon reaches its body length at more than 14 inches (36 cm). It is a medium-sized, compact built pigeon.  
Its tail is 4 to 5 inches (10 to 13 cm) long, while its beak measures less than 1 inch (2.5 cm). 
The males have a bright green head and chest, olive-colored wings, throat and neck, and a white belly, undertail and legs, like the name suggests. Its beak is a light bluish, and on the shoulders a patch of maroon is seen. The eye rings are vivid blue, the iris pink to violet. Their feet are red to violet.
The females, on the other hand, may look like the males, but are not as colorful. They have not maroon patches on their shoulders, and their breast is a frosted green.


Taxonomy
The white-bellied green pigeon is a member of the family Columbidae, which includes several subfamilies like Raphinae. This includes the tribe Treronini, and with it the genus Treron. It is closely related to the Ryukyu green pigeon.


Behaviour
The pigeon is known for its unusual habit of drinking saltwater. Scientists believe that this can help with the pigeon's digestion of  acidic berries. A well-known location where the pigeons do this in Japan is Terugasaki in Ōiso in Kanagawa Prefecture. White-bellied green pigeons live in pairs or in groups of up to ten individuals. They are also known to be shy and secretly living.

White-bellied green pigeons eat exclusively fruits, like cherries of the Korean cherry tree, grapes of the crismon glory wine, and cornels. In winter, they feed on acorns. They mostly pick the fruits from branches. Sometimes it comes to the ground to drink water. In Hokkaido, people watched pigeon groups of two hundred individuals, in Honshu groups of 440 individuals. The saltwater-drinking behavior is only seen in summer, which is why the summer diet lacks on minerals.
The pigeon’s nest is typical of pigeons built in trees as a loose platform of twigs. The females lay two eggs.

The '''white-bellied green pigeon''' ('''''Treron sieboldii'''''), also known as '''Siebold's green pigeon''', is a species of [[bird]] in the family [[Columbidae]]. It is found in [[East Asia|East]], and [[Southeast Asia]].<ref name="iucn status 12 November 2021"/> Its natural [[habitat]] is temperate [[forest]]s and woodlands. Its population has increased significantly since 2000.<ref>{{Citation |last=Ueta |first=Mutsuyuki |title=Avifauna at Ooyamazawa: Decline of Birds that Forage in Bushy Understories |date=2020 |work=Long-Term Ecosystem Changes in Riparian Forests |series=Ecological Research Monographs |pages=201–211 |place=Singapore |publisher=Springer Singapore |doi=10.1007/978-981-15-3009-8_11 |isbn=978-981-15-3008-1|doi-access=free }}</ref> It is one of the few green pigeons that migrates.

==Etymology==
The species [[epithet]] is named after [[Phillip Franz von Siebold]], a German naturalist and traveler.

==Distribution and habitat==
White-bellied green pigeons are found in [[Japan]], eastern [[China]] and northern [[Southeast Asia]]].<ref name = "HBW">{{Citation | first = Luis | last = Baptista | first2 = Pepper | last2 = Trail| first3 = Helen | last3 = Horblit |editor-first = Josep | editor-last = del Hoyo | editor2-first = Andrew | editor2-last = Elliott | editor3-last = Sargatal | editor3-first = Jordi | contribution = Family Columbidae (Pigeons and Doves | title = [[Handbook of the Birds of the World]]. Volume 3, Hoatzin to Auks | year = 1996 | page = 203 | place = Barcelona | publisher = Lynx Edicions | isbn = 84-87334-20-2 }}</ref> <ref name="iucn status 12 November 2021"/> In Japan, their population is found mainly in [[Hokkaido]] and the [[Ryukyu Islands]]. They also occur in [[Taiwan]] and eastern and southern China, near the coast, and in northern [[Thailand]], [[Laos]] and [[Vietnam]].

The species is generally sedentary, but birds in Hokkaido are wholly migratory and only present there from May to September, and the populations in the rest of Japan also undertake some poorly understood migrations.<ref name = "HBW"/> 

This pigeon is a forest dweller. It settles deciduous and mixed forests and prefers primary forests. In Japan, it exists in the forests of lowlands and in mountain forests, up to {{convert|1400|meter|abbr=on}}.<ref name = "HBW"/> 

==Description==
[[File:Columba (Vinago) sieboldii Fauna Japonica.jpg|thumb|White-bellied green pigeon, illustration]]
The white-bellied green pigeon reaches its body length at more than {{convert|14|in|cm}}. It is a medium-sized, compact built pigeon. <ref name="Gibbs454"> Gibbs, Barnes und Cox: ''Pigeons and Doves.'' S. 454.</ref> 

Its tail is {{convert|4|to|5|in|cm}} long, while its beak measures less than {{convert|1|in|cm}}. 

The males have a bright green head and chest, olive-colored wings, throat and neck, and a white belly, undertail and legs, like the name suggests. Its beak is a light bluish<ref name="Gibbs456"> Gibbs, Barnes und Cox: ''Pigeons and Doves.'' S. 456.</ref>, and on the shoulders a patch of maroon is seen. The eye rings are vivid blue, the iris pink to violet. Their feet are red to violet.

The females, on the other hand, may look like the males, but are not as colorful. They have not maroon patches on their shoulders, and their breast is a frosted green.

==Taxonomy==
The white-bellied green pigeon is a member of the family [[Columbidae]], which includes several [[subfamilies]] like Raphinae. This includes the [[Tribe (biology)|tribe]] Treronini, and with it the genus [[green pigeon|''Treron'']]. It is closely related to the [[Ryukyu green pigeon]].

==Behaviour==
The pigeon is known for its unusual habit of drinking saltwater. Scientists believe that this can help with the pigeon's [[digestion]] of  [[acidic]] [[berries]].{{Citation needed|date=October 2025}} A well-known location where the pigeons do this in Japan is Terugasaki in [[Ōiso, Kanagawa|Ōiso]] in [[Kanagawa Prefecture]].<ref>{{cite web |url=http://www.komatan.jp/e/aobato.html |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20040420200948/http://www.komatan.jp/e/aobato.html |archive-date=April 20, 2004 |title=Japanese Green Pigeon |author=<!--Not stated--> |access-date=March 21, 2018 }}</ref><ref>{{cite news |author=<!--Staff writer(s); no by-line.--> |title=White-bellied green pigeons battle waves in Kanagawa |url=http://mainichi.jp/graph/2015/09/04/20150904p2a00m0na005000c/001.html |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20180322015455/http://mainichi.jp/graph/2015/09/04/20150904p2a00m0na005000c/001.html |archive-date=March 22, 2018 |work=[[Mainichi Shimbun]] |date=November 4, 2015 |access-date=March 21, 2018 }}</ref> White-bellied green pigeons live in pairs or in groups of up to ten individuals. They are also known to be shy and secretly living.

[[File:Treron Sieboldii.jpg|thumb|White-bellied green pigeon eating a cherry]]
White-bellied green pigeons eat exclusively fruits, like cherries of the [[Prunus maximowiczii|Korean cherry tree]], grapes of the [[Vitis coignetiae|crismon glory wine]], and [[Cornus|cornel]]s. In winter, they feed on acorns. They mostly pick the fruits from branches. Sometimes it comes to the ground to drink water. In Hokkaido, people watched pigeon groups of two hundred individuals, in Honshu groups of 440 individuals. The saltwater-drinking behavior is only seen in summer, which is why the summer diet lacks on minerals.<ref name="Gibbs454"/>

The pigeon’s nest is typical of pigeons built in trees as a loose platform of twigs. The females lay two eggs.

zh.extract:
红翅绿鸠（学名：Treron sieboldii）又稱绿鸠、白嘴绿鸠、白腹綠鳩、白腹楔尾鸠或白腹楔尾绿鸠，为鸠鸽科绿鸠属的鸟类。分布于日本（主要四大島與琉球群島）、千島群島、庫頁島、朝鮮半島、中南半島、台灣以及中国大陆自秦岭至长江口以南范围、西至云南、河北等地，多见於山区的森林或多树地带。该物种的模式产地在日本。種名來自德國生物學家菲利普·法蘭茲·馮·西博德。


保护
中国国家重点保护动物等级：二级


亚种
红翅绿鸠佛坪亚种（学名：Treron sieboldii fopingensis）。中国的特有亞種，分布于陕西、四川、贵州等地。该物种的模式产地在陕西佛坪。
红翅绿鸠東南亚种（学名：Treron sieboldii murielae）。分布于泰國、越南以及中国大陆的广西、海南等地。该物种的模式产地在越南河内中部。
红翅绿鸠指名亚种（学名：Treron sieboldii sieboldii）。分布于日本、台灣以及中国大陆的河北、福建等地。该物种的模式产地在日本。
